title: Stable Transfection of Corallochytrium limacisporum Using Lonza Nucleofector
author: Aleksandra Kozyczkowska
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bpfgmjjw

steps.title: Inoculate 100 μl of C
steps.instruction: Inoculate 100 μl of C. limacisporum culture in 5ml of Marine Broth in 25cm 2 flask. Keep at 23°C air incubator for two days.

steps.title: Scrape gently and count cells to reach 1
steps.instruction: Scrape gently and count cells to reach 1.5E6 cells / condition using hemocytometer If there are more conditions, it is recommended to pull the cells together to get more visible pellet

steps.title: Spin down the cells at 1500xg...
steps.instruction: Spin down the cells at 1500xg for 5' Discard the supernatant.

steps.title: Wash the pellet with an ice-cold...
steps.instruction: Wash the pellet with an ice-cold 1XPBS (100 μl / condition) Spin down and discard the supernatant.

steps.title: Transfer the mix (cells + DNA...
steps.instruction: Transfer the mix (cells + DNA plasmid) into a strip (from the manufacture kit) Do it carefully & without creating bubbles Final volume: 20-25 μl

steps.title: Insert into Lonza nucelofection system and apply code
steps.instruction: Insert into Lonza nucelofection system and apply code: EN - 138

steps.title: After transfection, immediately add 80 μl...
steps.instruction: After transfection, immediately add 80 μl of Marine Broth medium (MB) to a well Mix up and down

steps.title: Check for positive cells 24 -...
steps.instruction: Check for positive cells 24 - 48h post-transfection using fluorescent microscopy

steps.title: Replace 500 μl of medium with...
steps.instruction: Replace 500 μl of medium with fresh MB that contains 300 μg/ml of puromycin Increase of mCherry-positive cells can be observed over time.

steps.title: Add DNA mix to the cell pellet
steps.instruction: Add DNA mix to the cell pellet

steps.title: It is recommended that plasmid is...
steps.instruction: It is recommended that plasmid is highly concentrated (2-5 μg/ul) to avoid final buffer dilution with the DNA solution Our plasmid contains mCherry coding sequence & resistance to puromycin gene ( pac ) under constitutive promoter: actin (Addgene: #

steps.title: Note
steps.instruction: Note: 10 μg of reporter plasmids guarantees the highest efficiency, however a successful transfection can be also obtained with 5 and 1μg of the plasmid

steps.title: Transfer DNA mix to 20 μl...
steps.instruction: Transfer DNA mix to 20 μl of ice-cold P3 buffer It is recommended not to keep the cells in P3 buffer for too long

steps.title: Transfer to 12-well plate (NUNC) that...
steps.instruction: Transfer to 12-well plate (NUNC) that contains 1 ml of growth medium (MB) and incubate overnight
